flashcards.front: What does IPsec stand for and what layer does it operate at?
flashcards.back: IP Security, operating at the Network Layer (Layer 3)

flashcards.front: What are the three main components of IPsec?
flashcards.back: Authentication Header (AH), Encapsulating Security Payload (ESP), and Internet Key Exchange (IKE)

flashcards.front: What is a Security Association (SA) in IPsec?
flashcards.back: A one-way logical connection that defines how security services will be applied to traffic

flashcards.front: What is the difference between transport mode and tunnel mode?
flashcards.back: Transport mode protects only the payload, while tunnel mode protects the entire original IP packet and adds a new IP header

flashcards.front: What does SPI stand for and what is its purpose?
flashcards.back: Security Parameter Index - it identifies the Security Association for a packet
